smells.name: Workflow Gate Missing
smells.trigger: A slice attempts to proceed or land without required stage evidence, landing verdict, or landing action evidence.

smells.name: Operator Input Boundary
smells.trigger: Required product direction, UAT, policy, business, explicit cost/risk, irreversible operational-risk, or genuinely ambiguous scope input is missing.

smells.name: Operator Escalation Overuse
smells.trigger: Operational drift, missing derivable metadata, malformed supported artifacts, projection mismatch, ledger drift, or workflow bookkeeping drift is escalated to the operator even though approved repo artifacts determine the intended state and no operator-owned gate is missing.

smells.name: Technical Question Escalation
smells.trigger: A workflow asks the operator to decide routine technical routing, implementation mechanics, agent/tool selection, skill scoping, review depth, test strategy, artifact structure, or Git plumbing when repo evidence and accepted policy are sufficient for Codex PM to decide.

smells.name: Mechanical Repair Overreach
smells.trigger: A mechanical repair path invents product scope, grants approvals, overrides policy, resolves ambiguous cost/risk, performs irreversible operational action, or force-resolves unsafe claim recovery.

smells.name: UAT Approval Boundary
smells.trigger: A feature slice needs UAT approval, has stale UAT, or changed code after approval.

smells.name: Evidence Freshness
smells.trigger: Tests, review evidence, UAT, landing verdicts, projections, or source-drift checks no longer satisfy the artifact-specific Evidence SLO for the current source.

smells.name: Evidence SLO Missing
smells.trigger: A cockpit, gate, review, landing, UAT, retrospective, or projection status is shown as trusted without an artifact-specific freshness rule, source artifact, owner, freshness state, or staleness reason.

smells.name: CodeRabbit Waiver Or Request Changes
smells.trigger: CodeRabbit has actionable comments, stale status, unresolved request changes, or a proposed waiver.

smells.name: Adversarial Review Required
smells.trigger: A PR or slice requires baseline local Qwen review or stronger review before landing.

smells.name: Security Privacy Auth Boundary
smells.trigger: Work touches authentication, authorization, secrets, privacy posture, trust boundaries, or sensitive data handling.

smells.name: Data Integrity Boundary
smells.trigger: Work changes durable records, canonical workflow state, migrations, destructive updates, or reconciliation behavior.

smells.name: Schema Or Migration Boundary
smells.trigger: Work adds or changes schemas, migration contracts, validation shape, or persisted artifact versions.

smells.name: Infra Deploy Release Billing Boundary
smells.trigger: Work touches infrastructure, deploys, releases, billing, paid services, or irreversible operational actions.

smells.name: Supply-Chain Boundary
smells.trigger: Work touches dependency manifests, lockfiles, package-manager scripts, CI or release workflows, agent skills, fetched prompts, external tool install paths, or other executable supply-chain surfaces.

smells.name: Layered Risk Classification Missing
smells.trigger: Review depth, operator supervision, or auto-landing eligibility is decided from smell triggers alone, or without hard never-auto-landable exclusions, blast-radius signals, static-analysis signals, source trust or input quarantine state, and supply-chain state.

smells.name: Never Auto-Landable Surface
smells.trigger: A change touches authentication, sessions, authorization, payment, billing, refunds, production data or schema migrations, secrets or credentials, CI or release workflow, dependency or fetched-prompt execution path, privacy, telemetry, export, destructive operations, or external side-effecting automation while still being considered auto-landable.

smells.name: High-Risk Signal Ignored
smells.trigger: A blast-radius, static-analysis, source-trust, input-quarantine, or supply-chain signal indicates elevated risk but review depth or landing eligibility is not changed because the smell list did not match.

smells.name: Untrusted Input Quarantine Missing
smells.trigger: A release-authorized agent path ingests external contributor text, issue or PR metadata, review comments, dependency documentation, fetched third-party content, generated instructions, or fetched prompts without data-only handling, an input quarantine boundary, source trust classification, and a trusted-source gate for any instruction-bearing use.

smells.name: Parser Validator Boundary
smells.trigger: Work changes parsers, validators, refusal paths, schema checks, or artifact readers and writers.

smells.name: JSONL Log Boundary
smells.trigger: Work changes JSONL append, replay, lifecycle event, or audit log behavior.

smells.name: Registry Index Authority Boundary
smells.trigger: Work adds registries, current-state views, caches, indexes, cockpit state, or UI state that might become hidden authority over append-only coordination history or repo-native artifacts.

smells.name: Coordination Projection Authority Boundary
smells.trigger: Work treats current-state views, in-flight registries, state indexes, cockpit status, or other projections as canonical workflow history, or allows them to grant active writable claims without CAS claim authority.

smells.name: Parallel Write Authority Boundary
smells.trigger: Work enables parallel writable workstreams, claim leases, work surface reservations, worktree lifecycle, or external side effects without CAS-backed claim authority, fencing-token enforcement, idempotency-key handling, work-surface wait-for graph cycle detection, and Git Mutation Serializer coverage for shared .git mutations.

smells.name: Example-Only Claim Safety Tests
smells.trigger: Release-authorized claim, release, reconcile, worktree-lock, or claim-gated side-effect correctness is proven only through hand-picked examples instead of declared Claim Safety Invariants plus fault-injecting or property-style claim simulation.

smells.name: File-Only Claim Authority
smells.trigger: A release-authorized writable claim is granted, renewed, released, completed, recovered, or treated as current through .bandit JSON, Markdown, JSONL, filesystem locks, worktree markers, or fail-closed reconciliation instead of the Git refs claim authority backend.

smells.name: Claim Idempotency Bypass
smells.trigger: A state-changing claim operation or claim-gated external side effect can run without an idempotency key, can reuse a key with different inputs, or can duplicate effects on retry or rerun.

smells.name: Work-Surface Deadlock Bypass
smells.trigger: Claimability or reservation logic only checks pairwise surface overlap and can miss a wait-for graph cycle between active reservations or claim candidates.

smells.name: Git Mutation Serializer Bypass
smells.trigger: A release-authorized parallel worktree path mutates shared .git plumbing through worktree add, remove, prune, lock or unlock, branch/ref maintenance outside claim CAS, or packed-refs-affecting maintenance without the CLI-owned Git Mutation Serializer, or creates a claim-owned worktree without an immediate claim-specific git worktree lock.

smells.name: Claim Worktree Unlocked
smells.trigger: A claim-owned worktree is created without git worktree lock, has a lock reason that does not name the Bandit claim ID, Work Item ID, and stage, includes a renewing fencing token in the lock reason, or can be unlocked by a worker path.

smells.name: Worktree Bootstrap Contract Missing
smells.trigger: A Bandit-created worktree is treated as runnable without a repo-native Worktree Bootstrap Contract covering allowed copied or linked files, setup commands, validation command, environment-variable references, secret-handling boundary, expected runtime dependencies, and bootstrap failure evidence.

smells.name: LLM Polling Scheduler Boundary
smells.trigger: Work uses LLM polling for ordinary no-op discovery or removes polling fallback without proving that available work still wakes the scheduler or agent path.

smells.name: Missing Agent Observability
smells.trigger: Work adds or changes agent execution, scheduler wakeups, claim operations, reviewer runs, tool calls, model calls, token spend, or retry/failure behavior without first-class trace evidence and canonical-artifact authority boundaries.

smells.name: Token Cost Failsafe Missing Or Too Strict
smells.trigger: Paid, high-token, reviewer, scheduler, or long-running agent execution lacks a soft budget band, provider-pricing evidence when paid, spend-class approval when paid, abnormal-run token-cost failsafe, trace-backed cost signal, or continuation path; or the budget cap is tight enough to force repeated failed attempts.

smells.name: Provider Pricing Evidence Missing
smells.trigger: Paid reviewer routing, paid model use, or paid/high-token execution relies on stale, missing, expired, or unversioned provider-pricing evidence, or lacks spend-class approval tied to current expected per-run cost.

smells.name: Paid Evaluation Spend Boundary
smells.trigger: A one-off paid reviewer or paid model call before policy promotion lacks benchmark/evaluation spend classification, per-run approval or active spend-class approval, or is used as recurring reviewer or model routing policy before the scoped promotion threshold is satisfied.

smells.name: Stage Capability Scope Missing
smells.trigger: A claimable stage starts without scoped skills and tools, references load-bearing skills without lifecycle contracts, or work introduces a new agent role only to express capability, model tier, reviewer depth, prompt behavior, or tool access rather than governed authority.

smells.name: Skill Lifecycle Contract Missing
smells.trigger: A load-bearing skill is required by stage policy, reviewer policy, model routing, or workflow automation without owner, version, changelog, intended stages, required tools, forbidden actions, evaluation packets, and rollback criteria.

smells.name: Generated Schema Boundary
smells.trigger: Work introduces generated schemas or generated artifacts that could obscure the maintained source contract.

smells.name: Large Scope
smells.trigger: A slice is broad enough to mix unrelated behaviors, artifacts, gates, or refactors.

smells.name: Multi-Phase Orchestration
smells.trigger: A single implementation path mixes planning, validation, subprocesses, writes, review, landing, or retrospective capture.

smells.name: Test Boundary Violation
smells.trigger: Implementation weakens Test Writer-owned assertions or changes tests without recorded test-change rationale.

smells.name: Missing Coverage
smells.trigger: Important behavior, refusal paths, state transitions, or evidence freshness checks lack tests or an explicit bootstrap gap.

smells.name: Repeated Repair Loop
smells.trigger: A slice needs repeated repairs for the same class of issue or exposes a recurring workflow failure.

smells.name: Workflow Trial Guardrails Missing
smells.trigger: A workflow trial, improvement evaluation, or workflow-policy change can become keep, revise, revert, or double_down policy without predeclared decision criteria, uncertainty or minimum-detectable-effect context, a re-evaluation window, and proxy-risk disposition.

smells.name: Context Pressure
smells.trigger: The task requires enough state, files, or history that future agents may lose the operating contract.

smells.name: Unavailable Agent Or Gate
smells.trigger: A required reviewer, agent, or integration does not exist yet during bootstrap.

smells.name: Malformed Evidence
smells.trigger: Required evidence exists but omits required fields, uses unsupported values, or cannot be parsed.

smells.name: Evaluation Policy Creep
smells.trigger: Agent evaluation or reviewer benchmarking changes live work routing, reviewer routing, model routing, skill policy, or cost policy without a separate approved policy decision.

smells.name: Reviewer Benchmark Scoring Missing
smells.trigger: Reviewer benchmark or paid-reviewer routing evidence lacks repo-derived failure-mode stratification, gold-labeled reviewer packets, seeded blockers, seeded non-issues, blocker-recall priority, actionable precision, useful finding yield, false-positive rate, tool friction, latency, or cost.

smells.name: Benchmark Holdout Missing
smells.trigger: Agent evaluation, reviewer routing, model routing, skill policy, or cost policy promotion relies on visible calibration packets without versioned locked holdout evidence.

smells.name: Paid Reviewer Promotion Threshold Missing
smells.trigger: Recurring paid reviewer routing is proposed or promoted without a risk-class or stage-capability-profile scope, predeclared threshold, locked-holdout blocker-recall improvement over Qwen, false-positive ceiling, provider-pricing-backed expected-cost ceiling, and operator-supervised spend-class approval.

smells.name: Policy Drift
smells.trigger: Implementation, templates, docs, or routing decisions diverge from AGENTS.md, CONTEXT.md, CLEAN_CODE.md, or stage rubrics.
